panels.description: Total number of observed nodes in the mesh

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(node_id) FROM node_details WHERE node_id <> '0' LIMIT 50

panels.title: Total nodes in mesh

panels.description: Nodes that sent any packet in the last 30 minutes
panels.title: Active nodes in the last 30 minutes

panels.description:
How many nodes are unknown and how many are known.

The unknown are ones that are either NULL in their long name or "Unknown" in their value

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
    COUNT(CASE WHEN long_name IS NULL OR long_name = 'Unknown' AND node_id != '0' THEN 1 END) AS "Unnamed",
    COUNT(CASE WHEN long_name IS NOT NULL AND long_name != 'Unknown' AND node_id != '0' THEN 1 END) AS "Named"
FROM
    node_details

panels.title: Nodes naming status

panels.description: Active = sent a packet in the last 30 minutes. Stale = seen before but quiet now.

targets.rawSql:
WITH last_seen AS (
    SELECT source_id AS node_id, MAX(time) AS t
    FROM mesh_packet_metrics
    GROUP BY source_id
)
SELECT
    COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN t > NOW() - INTERVAL '30 minutes' THEN 1 END), 0) AS "Active",
    COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN t <= NOW() - INTERVAL '30 minutes' THEN 1 END), 0) AS "Stale"
FROM last_seen

sql.whereString: (COALESCE(mqtt_status, '') <> '' AND mqtt_status NOT LIKE '%none%' AND mqtt_status NOT LIKE '%Unknown%')

panels.title: Node activity (last 30m)

panels.description: Total packets sent in specified time range

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS value
FROM mesh_packet_metrics
WHERE source_id IN (${Nodes:singlequote})
  AND $__timeFilter(time)

panels.title: Total packets sent

panels.description: Shows the total data sent on the mesh in bytes in the last hour. This includes all types of packets.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COALESCE(SUM(message_size_bytes), 0)::bigint AS value
FROM mesh_packet_metrics
WHERE source_id IN (${Nodes:singlequote})
  AND time >= NOW() - INTERVAL '1 hour'

panels.title: Data sent on mesh in last hour

panels.description: Shows the max recorded chanel utilization and the average

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
    $__timeGroupAlias(time, $__interval),
    AVG(channel_utilization) AS "Average Channel Utilization"
FROM device_metrics
WHERE node_id IN (${Nodes:singlequote})
  AND $__timeFilter(time)
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Channel Utilization (ChUtil)

panels.description:
This panel shows the percentage of airtime used for transmissions in the last hour. Airtime in LoRa networks represents the duration a device occupies the radio frequency channel to send data. It's a critical metric for:

Network capacity: Higher airtime usage indicates increased network load.

Regulatory compliance: Many regions limit the total airtime per device.

Battery life: More airtime generally means higher power consumption.

The data comes from Meshtastic packets, reflecting actual network usage. High percentages may suggest the need for optimization or capacity planning.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
    $__timeGroupAlias(time, $__interval),
    COALESCE(nd.long_name, dm.node_id) AS metric,
    AVG(dm.air_util_tx) AS value
FROM device_metrics dm
LEFT JOIN node_details nd ON nd.node_id = dm.node_id
WHERE dm.node_id IN (${Nodes:singlequote})
  AND $__timeFilter(dm.time)
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Duty Cycle (AirUtilTX)

panels.description: Top 10 nodes by average ChUtil over the selected range. Hover for live trend.

targets.rawSql:
WITH top_nodes AS (
    SELECT dm.node_id,
           AVG(dm.channel_utilization) AS util
    FROM device_metrics dm
    WHERE dm.node_id IN (${Nodes:singlequote})
      AND $__timeFilter(dm.time)
      AND dm.channel_utilization > 0
    GROUP BY dm.node_id
    ORDER BY util DESC
    LIMIT 10
)
SELECT
    $__timeGroupAlias(dm.time, $__interval),
    COALESCE(NULLIF(nd.long_name,'Unknown'), nd.short_name, dm.node_id) || ' (' || dm.node_id || ')' AS metric,
    AVG(dm.channel_utilization) AS value
FROM device_metrics dm
JOIN top_nodes tn ON tn.node_id = dm.node_id
LEFT JOIN node_details nd ON nd.node_id = dm.node_id
WHERE $__timeFilter(dm.time)
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Top 10 channel utilizers

panels.description:
Displays types of packets in the selected time range.

For more details see:
https://buf.build/meshtastic/protobufs/docs/main:meshtastic#meshtastic.PortNum

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
    NOW() AS time,
    portnum AS metric,
    COUNT(*)::float AS value
FROM mesh_packet_metrics
WHERE source_id IN (${Nodes:singlequote})
  AND $__timeFilter(time)
GROUP BY portnum
ORDER BY value DESC

panels.title: Packet types for selected time

panels.description: How many hops are configured for each client

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
    COALESCE(nd.long_name, m.source_id) AS metric,
    MAX(m.hop_start) AS value
FROM mesh_packet_metrics m
LEFT JOIN node_details nd ON nd.node_id = m.source_id
WHERE m.source_id IN (${Nodes:singlequote})
  AND $__timeFilter(m.time)
  AND m.hop_start > 0
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY value DESC

panels.title: The configured max hops for each client

panels.description: Graph that is built from Neighbor Info reports and shows the signal strenth for each line

targets.rawSql:
WITH pairs AS (
    SELECT source_id, destination_id
    FROM mesh_packet_metrics
    WHERE time > NOW() - INTERVAL '1 hour'
      AND destination_id NOT IN ('4294967295','1','0')
      AND source_id <> destination_id
),
connected AS (
    SELECT source_id      AS node_id FROM pairs
    UNION SELECT destination_id FROM pairs
    UNION SELECT node_id        FROM node_neighbors
    UNION SELECT neighbor_id    FROM node_neighbors
),
ranked AS (
    SELECT c.node_id, COUNT(p.*) AS conns
    FROM connected c
    LEFT JOIN pairs p
      ON p.source_id = c.node_id OR p.destination_id = c.node_id
    GROUP BY c.node_id
    ORDER BY conns DESC
    LIMIT 200
)
SELECT
    cd.node_id AS "id",
    cd.long_name AS "title",
    cd.short_name AS "subtitle",
    cd.hardware_model AS "detail__Hardware Detail",
    cd.role AS "detail__Client Role",
    cd.mqtt_status AS "detail__MQTT Status",
    CASE
        WHEN cd.mqtt_status = 'online' THEN '#2ECC71'
        WHEN cd.mqtt_status = 'offline' THEN '#E74C3C'
        ELSE '#808080'
    END AS "color"
FROM node_details cd
JOIN ranked r ON r.node_id = cd.node_id

WITH pairs AS (
    SELECT
        source_id,
        destination_id,
        COUNT(*) AS pkts,
        AVG(rx_snr)  AS avg_snr,
        AVG(rx_rssi) AS avg_rssi
    FROM mesh_packet_metrics
    WHERE time > NOW() - INTERVAL '1 hour'
      AND destination_id NOT IN ('4294967295','1','0')
      AND source_id <> destination_id
    GROUP BY source_id, destination_id
)
SELECT
    source_id || '_' || destination_id AS id,
    source_id      AS "source",
    destination_id AS "target",
    ROUND(avg_snr::numeric, 1) AS "mainstat",
    pkts                      AS "secondarystat",
    CASE
        WHEN avg_snr < -13 THEN '#E74C3C'
        WHEN avg_snr <  -7 THEN '#F4D03F'
        ELSE '#2ECC71'
    END AS "color",
    GREATEST(0.5, LEAST(4, LOG(pkts + 1))) AS "thickness"
FROM pairs

UNION ALL

SELECT
    neighbor_id || '_' || node_id AS id,
    neighbor_id AS "source",
    node_id     AS "target",
    snr         AS "mainstat",
    NULL        AS "secondarystat",
    CASE
        WHEN snr < -13 THEN '#E74C3C'
        WHEN snr <  -7 THEN '#F4D03F'
        ELSE '#2ECC71'
    END AS "color",
    GREATEST(0.5, LEAST(4, 1 + ((snr + 13) / 10))) AS "thickness"
FROM node_neighbors

panels.title: Node Graph

panels.description: Information stored on the Redis DB which includes "hard to get" information - that is needed to be gathered over time.
panels.title: General Information

panels.description: Displays the nodes on the map

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
    node_id,
    short_name,
    long_name,
    hardware_model,
    role,
    mqtt_status,
    longitude * 1e-7 AS longitude_norm,
    latitude  * 1e-7 AS latitude_norm,
    altitude
FROM node_details
WHERE longitude IS NOT NULL AND longitude <> 0

panels.title: Nodes map

list.definition:
SELECT 
  concat(long_name, ' (', node_id, ')') as __text, 
  node_id as __value 
FROM node_details 
ORDER BY long_name
list.label: Nodes
list.name: Nodes
list.query:
SELECT 
  concat(long_name, ' (', node_id, ')') as __text, 
  node_id as __value 
FROM node_details 
ORDER BY long_name
